name: quality-fixer
description: Automatically apply safe quality fixes including formatting (Black, isort), linting (Ruff auto-fixes), and resolve formatter conflicts. Use when quality checks fail or before committing code.
capabilities: Apply Black code formatting to Python files
Resolve Black vs Ruff formatter conflicts
Preview changes with dry-run mode before applying
Generate fix reports with git diff integration
author: Atyantik
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing instructions for standard Python development tools. All static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns: bash command examples (backticks), JSON metadata fields, and common words in descriptions. No executable code, network calls, credential access, or malicious patterns exist.
valueStatement: Manual code formatting takes time and creates inconsistent style across a codebase. This skill automatically applies Black formatting, isort import sorting, and Ruff linting fixes to keep code clean and consistent.
userTitle: Apply Python Code Formatting and Linting Fixes
limitations: Only fixes auto-fixable issues - manual review needed for complex linting
Does not fix logic errors or business logic problems
Does not write or improve docstrings automatically

useCases.title: Pre-commit Quality Check
useCases.description: Format and lint code before every commit to maintain consistent style

useCases.title: CI/CD Integration
useCases.description: Run local fixes when CI quality checks fail to resolve issues quickly

useCases.title: Codebase Cleanup
useCases.description: Clean up formatting issues across a large codebase efficiently

promptTemplates.title: Format All Files
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply quality fixes to the entire project using Black, isort, and Ruff. Run make format and make lint with --fix. Show me a summary of all changes made.

promptTemplates.title: Fix Specific File
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix quality issues in src/path/to/file.py. Apply Black formatting, isort, and Ruff auto-fixes. Show me the diff of changes.

promptTemplates.title: Preview First
promptTemplates.prompt: Preview quality fixes for src/ directory. Run all formatters and linters in dry-run mode (--check --diff). Show me exactly what would change without applying fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Resolve Conflicts
promptTemplates.prompt: Resolve Black and Ruff formatter conflicts in the project. Run both formatters, identify disagreements, and apply conflict resolution strategies like message variable extraction.

bestPractices: Run quality fixes before every commit to maintain consistent style across the codebase
Review changes with git diff instead of blindly accepting all auto-fixes
Run make test after fixing to ensure no breaking changes were introduced
antiPatterns: Applying fixes without reviewing what changed - always verify modifications
Ignoring formatter conflicts instead of resolving them properly
Relying on auto-fix for complex linting issues that require manual review

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: Yes. It runs Black, isort, and Ruff with --fix which will modify Python files in your project.

faq.question: What formatting tools does it use?
faq.answer: The skill uses Black for code formatting, isort for import sorting, and Ruff for linting auto-fixes.

faq.question: Can I preview changes before applying?
faq.answer: Yes. Use dry-run mode with --check --diff flags to preview all changes without modifying files.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-based skill that only runs standard Python development tools on your local project files.

faq.question: What if auto-fix breaks my code?
faq.answer: You can revert changes with git checkout -- <file>. The skill recommends committing before applying fixes.

faq.question: How is this different from running make commands?
faq.answer: This skill orchestrates all quality tools together, resolves conflicts between formatters, and provides a comprehensive report of all fixes applied.
